title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hangover

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with hangover because its natural compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds work to alleviate symptoms of a hangover, including nausea, headaches, and fatigue, by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Additionally, the tincture's calming effects on the nervous system can help to soothe an irritated stomach and promote relaxation, making it easier to recover from a night of overindulgence.
remedy_properties: Ginger: Anti-inflammatory and digestive enzymes that help soothe stomach upset and nausea.
Peppermint: Antispasmodic and calming properties that relieve headaches and dizziness.
Dandelion root: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid digestion, reduce bloating, and support liver function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to hangover-related discomfort.

Terpenoids (specifically, Ginkgotoxin): Ginkgotoxin, a terpenoid constituent of Ginkgo biloba, is believed to have a protective effect on the liver and can help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing the toxic effects of alcohol on the liver.

Bilobalide: This triterpene constituent of Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the brain from oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, potentially reducing hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which helps alleviate hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Seeds are sometimes used as an alternative to leaves, as they also contain flavonoids and terpenoids that can help reduce hangover severity.
Roots: Roots of Ginkgo biloba are also used, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, for their potential to alleviate hangover symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in early summer when the leaves are fully developed but still green.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 cup of the ginkgo biloba powder with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with hangover because it contains adaptogenic compounds that help the body cope with stress and anxiety, which are often exacerbated by excessive alcohol consumption. The tincture's ability to reduce cortisol levels and promote relaxation can alleviate symptoms of nausea, headaches, and fatigue commonly experienced after a night out. Additionally, ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe an irritated digestive system and reduce dehydration-related discomforts, making it a natural and effective remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps regulate stress hormones, reducing inflammation and fatigue caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Antioxidant activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties combat oxidative stress and cell damage caused by ethanol, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and nausea.

GABA-boosting effects: Ashwagandha increases GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and alleviating symptoms of hangovers such as jitteriness and irritability.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These steroidal lactones help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often exacerbated by excessive alcohol consumption.
Alkaloids (Withanine and Withanone): These compounds have a sedative effect, which can help counteract the stimulating effects of hangover, such as anxiety and restlessness.

Terpenoids (Withaferin A and Withanone): These compounds possess antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize the free radicals produced by alcohol metabolism, thereby reducing hangover-related damage to the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds such as withanolides, which are believed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves of Withania somnifera are also used to make tinctures for hangovers due to their adaptogenic properties, which can help the body adapt to stress and recover from hangover.

Seeds: Withania somnifera seeds are used to make tinctures for hangovers due to their high antioxidant content, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation associated with hangover.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh withania somnifera roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Chop the cleaned withania somnifera roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine the chopped withania somnifera roots with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts vodka.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with hangover because of its unique combination of properties. The tincure's flavonoids and anthocyanins have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, maypop contains alkaloids that have been shown to inhibit the activity of enzymes involved in alcohol metabolism, potentially reducing the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches. This natural remedy can provide a gentle yet effective way to alleviate hangover discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Maypop tinctures contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can lead to hangover symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tannins and flavonoids present in maypop have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation and discomfort associated with hangovers, such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue.

Sedative and calming effects: Maypop contains sedative and calming compounds like alkaloids and glycosides that can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress often experienced after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to mitigate the effects of hangovers by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Isoflavones: Isoflavones, specifically kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to possess antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue.

Harman alkaloids (e.g., harmine, harmaline): These alkaloids have a sedative effect and may help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and alkaloids, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as anxiety and nausea.
Fruits: The fruits contain serotonin and other compounds that may aid in reducing hangover symptoms such as headaches and mood swings.
Roots: The roots contain alkaloids and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and 2 parts of leaves by cutting them in the early morning.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer on paper bags in a warm well-ventilated area for 1 week.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar.
Fill a glass jar with 70 percent ethanol and cover the chopped plant material with the liquid by using 1 part of plant material to 3 parts of ethanol.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks and then strain and discard the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with hangover because of its unique properties that aid in soothing the stomach and alleviating digestive discomfort. The herb's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce nausea and vomiting, while its antimicrobial properties help eliminate toxins from the body. Additionally, highbush cranberry's ability to balance blood sugar levels can help stabilize energy and alleviate headaches associated with hangovers. As a natural remedy, it offers a gentle and effective way to combat the unpleasant symptoms of a night out.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Highbush cranberry tincture contains powerful antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Digestive soothing properties: The anthraquinones present in highbush cranberry tincture have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help soothe digestive issues like nausea, stomach pain, and diarrhea often experienced during a hangover.

Vasoconstricting properties: Highbush cranberry contains compounds that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, which may alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and headaches associated with hangovers.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hangover symptoms.
Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help alleviate hangover headaches and nausea by reducing inflammation in blood vessels and the digestive system.
Procyanidins: These complex tannins may help replenish the body's stores of antioxidants and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as fatigue and mental fogginess.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Vaccinium macrocarpon are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Vaccinium macrocarpon are used for their ability to aid in digestion and potentially reduce inflammation, which may help mitigate hangover effects.
Barks: The barks of Vaccinium macrocarpon are used due to their potential to reduce inflammation and provide antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon berries in the summer when ripe and fully purple.
Dry the berries in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 1 pound of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar every day to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with hangover because of its potent digestive properties. It stimulates liver function, enhancing the body's natural detoxification process to break down excess toxins and metabolites accumulated during excessive alcohol consumption. The bitter compounds in dandelion also help to stimulate appetite and alleviate nausea, reducing symptoms of a hangover such as stomach discomfort and headaches. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and pain associated with a hangover.
remedy_properties: Liver Support: Dandelion root contains compounds that have been shown to support liver function and promote detoxification, helping to remove toxins from the body and alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and fatigue.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Dandelion leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to hangover symptoms like headaches and digestive discomfort.

Diuretic Properties: Dandelion root has been shown to increase urine production, helping to flush out toxins and excess fluids from the body, which can be particularly beneficial for alleviating dehydration and bloating associated with hangovers.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Coumarins: Coumarins in dandelion tinctures may help reduce hangover symptoms by exhibiting diuretic properties, which aid in the removal of excess fluids and electrolytes from the body.

Phenolic acids (especially Chlorogenic acid): These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing liver damage and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale contain inulin, a type of fiber that can help regulate blood sugar levels and reduce hangover symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots and leaves of taraxacum officinale at the end of flowering stage with scissors.
Clean the plant material thoroughly with a brush to remove dirt and debris from the roots and leaves.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned plant material with 2 parts of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with hangover because of its calming properties that soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds also alleviate headaches and fatigue, making it a natural remedy for relieving hangover symptoms. Additionally, chamomile's sedative qualities can help promote a restful night's sleep, allowing the body to recover more efficiently from a night of heavy drinking. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, herbal chamomile tinctures provide a gentle yet effective way to alleviate hangover discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Gastroprotective Effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile tincture soothe the stomach and intestines, alleviating nausea, vomiting, and abdominal discomfort that often accompany hangovers.
Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Chamomile's calming effects help reduce anxiety, stress, and irritability caused by hangover symptoms, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of agitation.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps alleviate hangover symptoms by modulating the body's inflammatory response and acting as a natural anti-anxiety agent.
Luteolin: This flavonoid exhibits antioxidant properties, which help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangover.
β-Apinene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate hangover-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Matricaria chamomilla for making tinctures to alleviate hangover symptoms due to their high apigenin content, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Leaves: The leaves contain apigenin and other flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and calm the nervous system, making them a suitable ingredient in hangover tinctures.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla contain apigenin and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at a 1:3 ratio to a neutral solvent in late morning or early afternoon.
Combine 1 part of the dried flowers with 3 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and place it in a dark cool place for 2 to 3 weeks to steep and infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use allowing it to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with hangover because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate nausea and digestive issues commonly associated with overindulgence. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, making it easier for the body to absorb vital nutrients and fluids. Additionally, ginger's natural digestive properties help to settle the stomach and ease symptoms of dizziness and lightheadedness, allowing individuals to recover from a hangover more quickly and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce nausea, headaches, and soreness associated with hangovers.

Ginger's digestive stimulants: Ginger's natural oils can stimulate digestion and relieve stomach upset by increasing gastric motility, reducing inflammation, and relaxing the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, making it easier to digest food after a night of heavy drinking.

Ginger's antioxidant properties: Ginger contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help neutralize free radicals formed during alcohol metabolism, thereby reducing oxidative stress and damage to liver cells that can exacerbate hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol and [8]-gingerol, are known for their anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties, which help alleviate hangover symptoms such as stomach discomfort and nausea.
Shogaols: Shogaols are bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce hangover symptoms like pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress.

Terpenes (specifically, Zingiberene): Zingiberene, a terpene found in ginger, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as pain and nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for hangover tinctures due to their high gingerol content, which has anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used for hangover tinctures because they contain compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Stems: Stems of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used for hangover tinctures, as they contain some of the same active compounds as the rhyzomes and roots, although in lesser amounts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots with healthy appearance in late summer.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or food processor.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass container for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ivy gourd tinctures helps with hangover because it possesses natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of a hangover. The tannins present in ivy gourd have been shown to reduce blood vessel dilation, which can lead to headaches and flushing. Additionally, the tincture's ability to calm the stomach and regulate digestive functions can help ease nausea and discomfort. Furthermore, its adaptogenic properties may help rebalance the body's cortisol levels, reducing fatigue and stress associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ivy gourd contains flavonoids and other antioxidants that could help mitigate hangover symptoms caused by oxidative stress.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which might aid in reducing headache and discomfort associated with a hangover.

Digestive benefits: Ivy gourd has been traditionally used as a natural remedy for digestive issues like diarrhea, constipation, and stomach pain, which could potentially help alleviate symptoms of a hangover caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, thereby reducing hangover discomfort.

Phenolic Acids (e.g., Chlorogenic Acid): Phenolic acids, particularly chlorogenic acid, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help mitigate hangover symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue.

Tannins: Tannins in Coccinia grandis tinctures may help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut microbiome, which can become imbalanced due to excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits of Coccinia grandis are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce hangover symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves of Coccinia grandis are used for their purported diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may aid in reducing hangover symptoms and promoting recovery.
Roots: Roots of Coccinia grandis are used due to their possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of coccinia grandis leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested coccinia grandis into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped coccinia grandis with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with hangover because it is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, stress, and restlessness. The active compounds in kava, known as kavalactones, work to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of jitteriness and unease often experienced after a night of heavy drinking. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory properties can help ease headaches and stomach discomfort, making it an effective way to recover from a hangover.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kava's active compounds have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which can help reduce muscle tension, headaches, and anxiety associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal kava tinctures contain antioxidants that can help mitigate oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing the risk of liver damage and other hangover-related issues.

GABA-receptor stimulation: Kavalactones in kava tinctures bind to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and helping to regulate sleep patterns disrupted by a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_constituents: Kavain: Kavain helps with hangover by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce anxiety and stress associated with a hangover.
Dihydrokavain: Dihydrokavain helps with hangover by exhibiting anxiolytic and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate the muscle tension and anxiety that often accompany a hangover.
Yohimbine-free Harmsalactone: Harmsalactone may help with hangover by acting as an antagonist of the NMDA receptor, which can help alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with a hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also used, as they contain similar compounds to rhizomes, although in lower concentrations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh piper methysticum roots and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Chop the piper methysticum roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or tool.
Combine 1 part chopped piper methysticum roots with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tinctures helps with hangover because they contain concentrated plant extracts that work to alleviate symptoms of dehydration, nausea, and fatigue. Herbal teas like ginger, peppermint, and dandelion root can help soothe an upset stomach and calm the mind, reducing feelings of dizziness and disorientation. Additionally, certain herbs like ginseng and chamomile have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce hangover headaches and body aches. By providing a gentle and natural relief, herbal tea tinctures can help ease the discomfort of a hangover without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Digestive-soothing compounds: Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile contain compounds that soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate nausea and digestive discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Many herbal teas, such as green tea, ginkgo biloba, and milk thistle, are rich in antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, reducing hangover symptoms like fatigue and brain fog.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Herbs like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with hangovers, such as headaches and muscle aches.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), help with hangover by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, a type of polyphenol, may help alleviate hangover by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as modulating the activity of certain neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which can be imbalanced after excessive drinking.

L-Theanine: L-Theanine, an amino acid found in Camellia sinensis, may help with hangover by promoting relaxation and reducing stress and anxiety caused by hangover symptoms, such as headaches and nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Camellia sinensis leaves contain compounds like theophylline and caffeine, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms like fatigue and headaches.
Roots: Camellia sinensis roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce hangover-related inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: Camellia sinensis barks contain flavonoids that may aid in soothing hangover symptoms like nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest camellia sinensis leaves at the peak of freshness for optimal tincture quality and potency.
Clean and dry camellia sinensis leaves to remove any dirt or debris using a clean cloth.
Combine 250 grams of camellia sinensis leaves with 750 milliliters of 25% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort tinctures helps with hangover because it contains a unique blend of bioactive compounds that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms. The tannins in ironwort help to reduce inflammation and soothe an upset stomach, while the flavonoids improve blood flow and circulation. This combination can help to ease headaches, fatigue, and nausea often associated with hangovers, allowing individuals to recover faster and feel more refreshed. Additionally, ironwort's antioxidant properties may also help to mitigate damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ironwort tincture contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals formed during heavy alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms like inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ironwort's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body caused by excessive drinking, alleviating hangover symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and joint pain.

Digestive support: The tannins and flavonoids present in Ironwort tincture may help soothe and protect the digestive system from the irritation caused by alcohol consumption, potentially reducing nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ironwort roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Barks: Ironwort barks have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, which can be exacerbated by heavy drinking and may contribute to hangover discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sideritis scardica leaves at dawn when they are most potent and fragrant.
Dry the fresh sideritis scardica leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until completely dry.
Grind the dried sideritis scardica leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one part sideritis scardica powder with three parts 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 14 days shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with hangover because of its unique ability to calm the nervous system, alleviating symptoms such as dizziness, anxiety, and irritability. The herbal extract acts as a natural relaxant, soothing an overactive brain and promoting a sense of calmness and tranquility. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns, allowing the body to recover more efficiently from the previous night's indulgences. As a result, taking an herbal valerian tincture can provide welcome relief from hangover symptoms, allowing individuals to bounce back quickly and feel revitalized.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative effects can help to reduce anxiety, jitteriness, and irritability often associated with hangovers.
Stomach-soothing compounds: Valerian contains compounds like isovaleric acid, which can help to calm stomach upset, nausea, and digestive discomfort caused by excessive drinking.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidants may help to neutralize the free radicals generated by alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their high content of valerenic acid, which helps alleviate symptoms associated with hangovers.
Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the valerian plant and contains a concentrated amount of essential oils that help soothe hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried valeriana officinalis roots or rhizomes and chop them into smaller pieces for infusion.
Combine the chopped valeriana root with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place for up to one year and administer 20-30 drops sublingually as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops tinctures helps with hangover because of their unique ability to calm the mind and body. The active compounds in hops, such as humulone and lupulone, have been shown to reduce anxiety and inflammation, which are common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, hops have been found to promote relaxation and reduce irritability, making it easier to recover from a night of excessive drinking. By using an herbal hops tincture, individuals can experience relief from hangover symptoms and regain their normal state more quickly.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: Hops contain flavonoids and humulone, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and recover from a night of heavy drinking.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Hops' antioxidant compounds, such as polyphenols and sesquiterpenes, help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate hangover symptoms like headaches, nausea, and fatigue.

Gastrointestinal soothing properties: The volatile oils in hops, particularly humulone, have anti-inflammatory effects on the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to hangover symptoms like stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, hop roots can help soothe and calm the digestive system after a night of excessive drinking.
Barks: The bark of the hops plant contains compounds that can aid in digestion and reduce inflammation, making it an effective remedy for hangover relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hops flowers humulus lupulus by cutting fresh blooms in mid-morning when dew has dried.
Dry the harvested hops flowers by spreading them thinly on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Prepare a menstruum solution by mixing 60% vodka and 40% distilled water with a ratio of 1 part hops to 4 parts liquid.
Combine the dried hops flowers with the prepared menstruum solution in a glass jar and steep at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids before storing it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with hangover because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea and stomach cramps, while its antioxidant properties work to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate hangover symptoms. Additionally, aloe tinctures can also help to replenish lost fluids and electrolytes, promoting hydration and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms like headaches and nausea caused by hangover.

Digestive soothers: Herbal aloe tincture contains anthraquinones, which have natural laxative properties that can help alleviate digestive discomfort, bloating, and stomach cramps associated with hangovers.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Leaves: Aloe leaves contain a compound called aloin, which has natural anti-diarrheal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe stomach upset and headaches associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves and rinse with distilled water.
Combine the harvested aloe vera gel with 20% ethanol or vodka in a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids before transferring to glass bottles.
Label and store the tincture at room temperature for up to 2 years or in the refrigerator for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with hangover because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed, curcuminoids in turmeric tincture reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption. This alleviates symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea often associated with hangovers. Additionally, turmeric's natural antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid and soothe digestive issues. As a result, taking an herbal turmeric tincture before or after consuming alcohol can significantly reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce hangover symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and nausea.
Gingerol: Gingerol, a bioactive compound in turmeric, has natural anti-nausea properties that can help alleviate stomach upset and dizziness commonly associated with hangovers.

Volatile Oils: Turmeric tincture's volatile oils, including cineole and eucalyptol, have been shown to possess analgesic and sedative effects, which may help soothe headache pain and promote relaxation after a night of heavy drinking.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots are commonly used in herbal remedies due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as curcuminoids, which help alleviate hangover symptoms.

Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of turmeric and contains a higher concentration of curcumin than other parts of the plant, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal tinctures for hangover relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots, cutting them into small pieces for immediate use or drying for future use.
Combine 30g of dried turmeric root with 200ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, sealing the lid tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the curcuma longa tincture to smaller dark glass bottles, labeling and dating each bottle for future use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with hangover because it contains compounds that target the main symptoms of a hangover, such as nausea, fatigue, and headaches. The calming effects of lemon balm can soothe an upset stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting, while its antispasmodic properties may help alleviate headache pain. Additionally, the herb's natural sedative properties can promote relaxation and reduce feelings of irritability and anxiety often associated with hangovers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidants help neutralize the damage caused by free radicals produced during alcohol metabolism, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangovers.

Gastric soothers: The volatile oils present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate nausea, stomach cramps, and digestive discomfort often experienced after excessive drinking.

Sedative and calming properties: Lemon balm's natural sedative compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help regulate the body's response to stress and anxiety, reducing hangover symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve liver function, helping to alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop 30 grams of harvested leaves into small pieces and combine with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 14 days shaking the jar twice daily to extract constituents.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Store the finished tincture in an amber glass bottle at room temperature and use within 2 years taking 20-30 drops as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with hangover because they contain natural compounds that can alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue. Additionally, the calming effects of beach rose tincture can soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation, making it easier to recover from a night of overindulgence. This natural remedy can provide quick relief and support for a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Beach Rose tincture helps reduce inflammation caused by excessive drinking, which can alleviate headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with hangovers.

Digestive soothing properties: The tincture's ability to calm digestive issues like stomach upset, diarrhea, and nausea helps mitigate the discomfort and misery often experienced after a night of heavy drinking.

Antioxidant and adaptogenic properties: Beach Rose contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals caused by alcohol consumption, while its adaptogenic properties support the body's natural detoxification processes, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall recovery from a hangover.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the beach rose contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate headaches and soothe stomach upset associated with hangovers.
Barks: The barks of the beach rose have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers in morning dew for maximum potency.
Dry rosa rugosa flowers at room temperature or using food dehydrator within hours.
Chop dried rosa rugosa flowers into small pieces and combine 1 part with 5 parts vodka in dark glass jar.
Steep mixture for 6 weeks in cool dark place, shaking jar every day to extract active compounds.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard solids, bottle remaining liquid and store in cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals generated by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hangovers.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain caused by hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue.
Gastroprotective properties: Kansas Coneflower's flavonoids and phenolic acids soothe the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation associated with hangover-related nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and anti-inflammatory effect, helping to alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
Barks: The barks contain salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues commonly experienced during hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in the early morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped echinacea angustifolia roots with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a large glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with hangover because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea. The tincture's flavonoids and phenolic acids also have antioxidant effects, neutralizing the oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Additionally, Kansas coneflower has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, making it an effective remedy for hangover-related stomach upset and discomfort.

American ginseng tinctures helps with hangover because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. The tincture's adaptogenic properties also aid in restoring balance to the body after a night of excessive drinking, allowing for a quicker recovery. Additionally, American ginseng has been shown to improve mental clarity and reduce stress, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating hangover symptoms and promoting a speedy return to normal functioning.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The saponin-rich extract of American Ginseng helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which contributes to hangover symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Antioxidant activity: American Ginseng's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals generated by ethanol metabolism, thereby protecting cells from damage and reducing the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.

Adaptogenic effects: The ginsenosides present in American Ginseng tinctures help adapt the body to stress (in this case, the aftereffects of excessive drinking), promoting a sense of balance and resilience, which can alleviate symptoms like anxiety, irritability, and mental fogginess.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like ginsenosides, which help alleviate hangover symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce headache and body aches associated with hangovers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried american ginseng root extract with a minimum of 4% ginsenosides.
Combine 10 grams of the extracted powder with 100 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and allow it to mature for at least 2 weeks before use.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hangover are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have natural healing properties. These tinctures can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover, such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea, by soothing the digestive system and replenishing lost nutrients. For example, ginger and peppermint tinctures can ease stomach discomfort, while passionflower and skullcap tinctures can calm anxiety and promote relaxation. By using herbal tinctures for hangover, individuals can reduce the severity of their symptoms, recover faster, and start their day feeling refreshed and revitalized.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal tinctures are various forms of anxiety, stress, and fatigue. For example, a blend of herbs such as passionflower, lemon balm, and chamomile can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Other herbal tinctures, like those containing ginseng, ashwagandha, or rhodiola rosea, may help combat fatigue and exhaustion. These natural remedies can provide relief from the physical and mental discomfort associated with hangover-like symptoms without the negative side effects of pharmaceutical medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with hangover is a blend of Milk Thistle, Ginger, and Peppermint. Milk Thistle's silymarin helps repair liver damage, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties alleviate nausea and headaches. Peppermint's digestive benefits soothe stomach discomfort. Additionally, consider adding Dandelion Root tincture to replenish lost electrolytes and support kidney function. This harmonious blend may help mitigate hangover symptoms, promoting a smoother recovery and reduced discomfort.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the aqueous extracts from Panax ginseng (PG) and Hippophae rhamnoides (HR) (AEPH) were found to be effective in treating acute alcohol intoxication in mice. The study involved 144 male Kunming mice, divided into six groups, where AEPH was administered in different dosages (100mg/kg, 200mg/kg, 400mg/kg) and the results showed that AEPH significantly prolonged alcohol tolerance time, shortened sober-up time, and decreased blood ethanol levels. These findings suggest that AEPH may be beneficial in alleviating hangover symptoms by increasing the activities of enzymes that metabolize alcohol in the liver and reducing the concentration of substances that contribute to hangover in the brain.
